steps.title: Subtract 20 from both sides to isolate the fraction
steps.content:
Start with the given equation: $p = 20 + \frac{16}{n}$

Subtract 20 from both sides:
$$p - 20 = \frac{16}{n}$$

steps.title: Solve for n by multiplying and dividing
steps.content:
From $p - 20 = \frac{16}{n}$, multiply both sides by $n$:
$$n(p - 20) = 16$$
Divide both sides by $(p - 20)$:
$$n = \frac{16}{p - 20}$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
